channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What Americans think about Hegseth, Gabbard and key Trump Cabinet picks: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As several of President-elect Donald Trump’s choices for high-level positions in his incoming administration face scrutiny on Capitol Hill, a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research shows that Americans have their own doubts.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: About 3 in 10 are highly confident in Trump on Cabinet, spending or military oversight: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans may have recently elected Donald Trump to a second term, but that doesn't mean they have high confidence in his ability to choose well-qualified people for his Cabinet — or effectively manage government spending, the military and the White House, according to a new poll f
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A nonprofit leader, a social worker: Here are the stories of the people on Biden's clemency list
item.description: Among the roughly 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted or who were pardoned by President Joe Biden are a nonprofit leader who supports at-risk youth, a social worker who fosters animals and a counselor and recovery coach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top GOP rebel angles for powerful House leadership-backed committee post
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy is floating himself as a possible candidate to chair the influential House Rules Committee next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mitt Romney's Senate exit may create a vacuum of vocal, conservative Trump critics
item.description: Sen. Mitt Romney is due to give his final speech after seeing the Republican Party for which he was once the standard bearer drastically transform under Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House releases national strategy to combat Islamophobia
item.description: The White House has announced what it called the first-ever national strategy to counter Islamophobia.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NYC to fund guaranteed income program: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City will provide funding for a guaranteed income program to support soon-to-be mothers experiencing housing insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Senate begins final push to expand Social Security benefits for millions of people
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is pushing toward a vote on legislation that would provide full Social Security benefits to millions of people, setting up potential passage in the final days of the lame-duck Congress. Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to next month's inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is breaking with precedent in inviting a foreign head of state to his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz says he’ll propose anti-fraud measures, reflects on VP bid
item.description: In his first interview with MPR News since his vice presidential run, Tim Walz said he still has “privilege” of serving as Minnesota governor, urged lawmakers to work together in divided government.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Ex-FBI informant accused of lying about Bidens agrees to plead guilty to federal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former FBI informant accused of falsely claiming that President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepted bribes has agreed to plead guilty to federal charges, according to court papers filed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: READ: Here is Biden's full list of clemencies
item.description: President Biden early Thursday announced that he is pardoning 39 people and commuting the sentences of nearly 1,500 others, setting a new daily record for clemency with a focus on those who were under home confinement during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Balluck, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China denies new report linking CCP to four sites in Cuba allegedly used to spy on the US
item.description: The Center for Strategic and International Studies released a report last week detailing facilities in Cuba that it claims China may be using to gather signal intelligence on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell working from home after tripping at Capitol
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) missed his usual morning speech to open the Senate on Thursday after falling earlier in the week and suffering a minor cut to the face and a sprained wrist.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House sends Biden bill adding judicial seats after veto threat
item.description: The House approved a bill Thursday that would create dozens of additional judicial seats over the next several years, sending it to President Biden’s desk after his administration levied a veto threat.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: The "Donald J. Trump Election Qualification Act" would grant the possibility of those who have been guilty of a felony charge to run.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences for 1,500 people, the largest act of clemency in a day
item.description: The 1,500 people had been serving long prison sentences that would have been shorter under today's laws and practices. Three Minnesota women are among those to get their federal convictions pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Q&A: Ben Wikler running for DNC chair
item.description: Ben Wikler is chair of the Democratic Party of Wisconsin and candidate for chair of the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Teacher sues Beverly Hills school district for racism after Trump win
item.description: The lawyer told NewsNation there were several specific incidents, including a video showing students mocking Black students in the school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senators urge Biden to act on temporary protections for migrants
item.description: The senators have been quietly urging the White House to take executive actions that would attempt to extend legal protections for migrants into Trump's administration, and the White House has been discussing what steps to take.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, COLLEEN LONG and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cornyn warns Hegseth faces 'unpleasant' confirmation hearing
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas) says Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s pick to head the Department of Defense, faces a nasty Senate confirmation hearing that would likely be uglier than the battle over Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump return to DC looms over Kennedy Center Honors
item.description: In a night filled with political moments — including multiple nods to President Joe Biden, a Grateful Dead tribute from former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC mayor will not completely rule out switch to Republican Party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York Democratic Mayor Eric Adams sent shockwaves through the political scene Friday morning after refusing to rule out running for another term as a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
